title: Debt-basics module (interest/principal/compounding/prioritization/cost of borrowing)
context: The Debt-basics module is the most risk-sensitive content module: it must explain interest, principal, compounding, debt prioritization, and the cost of borrowing clearly and accurately while never recommending specific loans, 'good debt' purchases, or debt-settlement services. Learners in debt distress must be routed to free nonprofit counseling — never to commercial debt-settlement or 'credit-repair' services, which are a predatory product category. This module is gated on a credentialed reviewer being engaged and the Budgeting module being complete.
objective: Draft, cite, plain-language review, accessibility review, linter-clean, and credentialed-expert-sign-off the Debt-basics module covering interest, principal, compounding, prioritization, and cost of borrowing — with debt-distress routing to free nonprofit help only.
acceptanceCriteria: Explains interest, principal, compounding, and debt prioritization with labeled illustrative examples; never recommends a specific loan, lender, or 'good debt' purchase

Debt-distress content routes learners to free nonprofit financial counseling (e.g., NFCC member agencies, nonprofit credit counselors) — never to commercial debt-settlement, 'credit-repair', or debt-consolidation services
All factual and numeric claims cited to vetted openly-licensed authoritative sources; numbers shown as labeled examples; currency shown neutrally

Linter-clean: no product/brand/affiliate/advice phrasing; no unflagged jurisdiction-specific terms (e.g., specific regulatory limits labeled by jurisdiction); 'Educational information, not financial advice' framing and non-commercial redirect present
Readability at or below grade 8; plain-language review passed; WCAG 2.2 AA met; a11y review passed
MDX frontmatter conforms to the content JSON Schema: sources[], reviewer, reviewedVersion, signoffDate, jurisdiction='neutral', notAdviceFraming:true, license=CC-BY-4.0
Credentialed financial-educator sign-off recorded for the shipped version; strings externalized for i18n
output: A reviewed, signed-off Debt-basics module (content/lessons/debt-basics.mdx) with full provenance metadata, linter-clean, accessible, plain-language, and translation-ready.
